title: Cantharospermum trineurum
intro: I was unable to find any information on the plant Cantharospermum trineurum.
taxonomy: Cantharospermum trineurum is classified in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the order Sapindales, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification also places it in the clade Eurosids I. At the kingdom level, it falls under Plantae. Cantharospermum trineurum is a species of flowering plant.
constituents: Cantharospermum trineurum has been reported to contain flavonoids and triterpenes, which are known to exhibit various biological activities. The flavonoids present in this plant are believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The triterpenes, on the other hand, are thought to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. However, the exact composition and concentration of these active constituents can vary depending on the plant's habitat and extraction methods. Further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological potential of Cantharospermum trineurum.
